title: Take a language test - Canada.ca
content:
## Take a language test
You need a certain level in English or French to qualify for the pilots. You need a level 4 in the Canadian Language Benchmark (CLB) or Niveaux de compétence linguistique canadiens (NCLC).
You’ll need to take an approved language test and include the results in your application. To do this,
- schedule your test with an approved agency
After you get the results of your test, compare them to the charts below to see what CLB or NCLC level you have.

## What to include in your application
Include a copy of your test results that shows you obtained level 4 in the CLB or NCLC in all 4 abilities. Your test results must be less than 2 years old when you apply.
Keep a copy of the report for your records and future use.

## Language tests we accept
You can take any of these approved language tests:
- CELPIP: Canadian English Language Proficiency Index Program You need to take the CELPIP-General test.
- You need to take the CELPIP-General test.
- IELTS: International English Language Testing System You need to take the IELTS General Training test.
- You need to take the IELTS General Training test.
- PTE: Pearson Test of English You need to take the PTE Core test.
- You need to take the PTE Core test.
- TEF Canada: Test d’évaluation de français
- TCF Canada : Test de connaissance du français

## How to find your language level based on your test results
Use your test results to find your CLB/NCLC level in the tables below:
